issues.blurb: Sealant on the roof, seams, and slide-outs dries out and cracks with age, letting water reach the wood substructure and cause hidden rot.
Slide-out motors, gears, cables, and seals wear or bind over time, leading to rooms that stick, leak, or fail to extend and retract evenly.
Hydraulic leveling jacks develop fluid leaks, failed solenoids, or sensor faults that leave the coach unable to level or stow the jacks.
Gas chassis (often the Ford V10) and diesel pushers see drivetrain, exhaust manifold, and overheating issues, especially when running heavy on grades.
Power converters, inverters, and auto-generator-start systems fail or behave erratically, disrupting 12V house power and battery charging.
Motorhome tires often time out before they wear out, and aged sidewalls blow on the highway even with plenty of tread remaining.

narrative.headline: What Class A motorhome owners report most
narrative.summary: These are issues commonly reported by Class A owners as a general reference, not expert-verified data and possibly outdated. Water intrusion, slide-out mechanisms, and leveling hydraulics tend to come up most across big motorhomes.

issues.title: Water Intrusion & Roof Leaks
issues.blurb: Commonly reported (~26%) — aging roof and seam sealant lets water in and rots the substructure if not caught early.

issues.title: Slide-Out Mechanism Failures
issues.blurb: Commonly reported (~22%) — motors, gears, and seals wear so rooms stick, leak, or extend unevenly.

issues.title: Leveling Jacks & Hydraulics
issues.blurb: Often reported (~18%) — fluid leaks and failed solenoids leave the coach unable to level or stow.

issues.title: Chassis & Engine
issues.blurb: Reported (~14%) — gas (Ford V10) and diesel chassis see drivetrain and overheating issues under heavy loads.

issues.title: House Electrical (Converter / Inverter)
issues.blurb: Reported (~12%) — converters, inverters, and auto-gen-start systems fail and disrupt 12V house power.

issues.title: Tire Age Blowouts
issues.blurb: Reported (~8%) — RV tires age out before the tread wears, and old sidewalls blow on the road.
